is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of Plasmodium falciparum DHFR and DHPS mutations; samples were microscopically confirmed P. falciparum malaria.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports Pfdhfr 51, 59, 108; Pfdhps 436, 437, 540, 581, 613 — all curated target codons.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Figures 2, 3, 6 give percent prevalence with N samples tested per site (e.g. Mali N=58, Kenya N=56, Malawi N=54, Bolivia N=59 for DHFR).

sub_country_location.evidence: African sites are sub-national: two clinics on outskirts of Bamako (Mali, 1995), district hospital in Kilifi coastal Kenya (1993), a clinic in town of Karonga Malawi (1995-1996).

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Mali collected 1995, Kenya 1993, Malawi 1995 and 1996 (Karonga), each within a <=3 year window.

african_field_samples.evidence: Fingerstick blood from persons presenting to clinics with confirmed P. falciparum malaria in Mali, Kenya, Malawi (Bolivia is South American and excluded).

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical PCR surveys conducted in four countries with mutation-specific PCR and sequencing.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Mali data overlaps with ref 23 (Djimde et al abstract, Am J Trop Med Hyg 1996) and prior Mali work (refs 1,10); Kenya samples relate to ref 22 (Khan et al). Some site data likely reported elsewhere.

supplement.evidence: Per-site frequencies and sample counts are given directly in Figures 2, 3, and 6.

assessment.summary: Primary PCR survey of P. falciparum antifolate resistance mutations (DHFR 51/59/108, DHPS 436/437/540/581/613) at sub-national African sites: Bamako Mali (1995), Kilifi Kenya (1993), Karonga Malawi (1995-96), plus Bolivia (excluded, non-African). Extractable per-site frequencies with N given in figures. Eligible; some duplicate risk with related Mali/Kenya publications.
